title: trigger preDisplay
text: trigger preDisplay System trigger that is executed before a menu or menu item is displayed. Declaration: trigger preDisplay Applies to: Dropdown menu , Menu Item Activation: Activated for each item on a menu (or menu bar) before that menu is displayed. The trigger is also activated if a keyboard accelerator is used. For details, see Trigger Activation . Default behavior: If no ProcScript is present in this trigger, there is no action. Behavior upon completion: None Description Use this trigger to enable or disable the menu item. You can affect the appearance of the menu item or to determine its current status using the ProcScript functions $check , $disable , and $hide . For dynamic menus (inline menus), use the preDisplay trigger to set the contents of the menu. The trigger must contain an $inlinemenu instruction that generates dynamic menu items. You should not use statements that change the active path or that affect the screen in this trigger. In addition, you should not include long operations in this trigger because the menu focus may have been lost earlier. Trigger Activation The preDisplay trigger is activated for each item on a menu (or menu bar) before that menu is displayed. The trigger is also activated if a keyboard accelerator is used. For a horizontal menu bar, the preDisplay trigger of each menu in the menu bar is activated when the menu bar is activated. This can be either in response to a ^PULLDOWN function or when the form is initially displayed due to an edit or display statement. For other menus and menu items, the preDisplay trigger for each menu item is activated when the menu is initially selected. For efficiency reasons, the trigger is not reactivated while menus are still active. Related Links $check Return or set the checked status of a menu item. $disable Disables a menu item in a menu object. $hide Return or set the display status of a menu item. $inlinemenu Insert or retrieve one or more menu items at the location of a dynamic menu placeholder trigger option Trigger for handling the processing that should occur after a menu item is selected.
